query: when was the Eiffel Tower built and finished
answer: The Eiffel Tower was built in Paris from 1887 to 1889 and officially opened on March 31, 1889. Construction took 2 years, 2 months, and 5 days.

results.snippet: # Eiffel Tower Construction : when was the eiffel tower built ? ## Date and major stages of construction of the Eiffel Tower. ### When was the Eiffel Tower built ? The Eiffel Tower was built for the Universal Exposition of 1889, which celebrated the centennial of the French Revolution. This ambitious, controversial project later became a quintessential feature of the Parisian skyscape and a symbol of the French capital around the world. The plan to build the Eiffel Tower was initially a response to a competition published in the Official Journal to prepare for the Universal Exposition of 1889. ## A tower built to last. The question is for how many years was the Eiffel Tower built ? Indeed, the landmark was built for the 1889 Universal Exposition in **Paris** and it had been commissioned as a temporary edifice. results.snippet: # Eiffel Tower. The **Eiffel Tower** (/ˈaɪfəl/ ⓘ *EYE-fəl*; French: *Tour Eiffel*  ⓘ) is a lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. During its construction, the Eiffel Tower surpassed the Washington Monument to become by far the tallest human-made structure in the world, a title it held for 41 years until the Chrysler Building in New York City was finished in 1930. According to *The New York Times*, "[at] a time when the [CIC] was helping finance one of the world's best-known landmarks, the Eiffel Tower, as a monument to French liberty, it was choking Haiti's economy, taking much of the young nation's income back to Paris and impairing its ability to start schools, hospitals and the other building blocks of an independent country.". results.title: Eiffel Tower opens in Paris | March 31, 1889 - History.com
results.snippet: # Eiffel Tower opens in Paris. On March 31, 1889, the Eiffel Tower is dedicated in Paris in a ceremony presided over by Gustave Eiffel, the tower’s designer, and attended by French Prime Minister Pierre Tirard, a handful of other dignitaries and 200 construction workers. Out of more than 100 designs submitted, the Centennial Committee chose Eiffel’s plan of an open-lattice wrought-iron tower that would reach almost 1,000 feet above Paris and be the world’s tallest man-made structure. Unperturbed, Eiffel completed his great tower under budget in just two years. The elevators were not completed by March 31, 1889, however, so Gustave Eiffel ascended the tower’s stairs with a few hardy companions and raised an enormous French tricolor on the structure’s flagpole. The Eiffel Tower remained the world’s tallest man-made structure until the completion of the Chrysler Building in New York in 1930.

results.snippet: # Origins and Construction of the Eiffel Tower. ## The Design of the Eiffel Tower. All the metal pieces of the tower are held together by rivets, a well-refined method of construction at the time the Tower was constructed. It only took **five months** to build the foundations and **twenty-one** to finish assembling the metal pieces of the Tower. The "Protest against the Tower of Monsieur Eiffel", **published in the newspaper Le Temps**, is addressed to the World's Fair's director of works, Monsieur Alphand. ## Excerpt from the "Protest Against Mr. Eiffel's Tower," 1887. > “We come, as**writers, painters, sculptors, architects, and passionate lovers of the beauty** that Paris has until now preserved intact, to protest with all our strength, with all our indignation, in the name of disregarded French taste, in the name of threatened French art and history, against the erection, in the very heart of our capital, of the useless and monstrous Eiffel Tower, which public malice, often marked by common sense and a sense of justice, has already dubbed the Tower of Babel.
